title: Video Script Schema
description: Schema for validating the 04-script structured block. As of the 03+04 merge, this block also carries the visual blueprint that previously lived in 03-plan-bilibili: optional top-level video_spec / zoom_crop_directives / template_tickets and per-section props / animation_cues / sub_shots. Stages 05 (B-roll) and 07 (assembly) read these directly from here. The shot layer (sections[].shots[]) is the renderable storyboard unit: a section is a narrative beat (one continuous voice paragraph), while each shot binds ONE component instance + props + the voice slice it covers + its own duration, so a long section is cut into several shots instead of one static picture (see shared/docs/remotion-spec.md §1.5).

anti_hype_forbidden.description: Marketing/clickbait phrases the 04 script bans; downstream stages (TTS, assembly, subtitle) must not re-introduce them. Enforced by scripts/pipeline_lint.py.

video_spec.description: Platform video spec. Merged in from the retired 03 stage; optional for back-compat with pre-merge episodes.

scene_template.description: Section-level single Remotion component, for SHORT single-shot sections only (duration_hint_seconds <= 15). When a section is cut into multiple shots, leave this empty and put one scene_template PER shot in shots[]; do not pick one component here and hide the rest in visual_instructions prose. Optional.

props.description: Section-level static Remotion props for the single-shot case. When shots[] is used, props live per shot instead. Data must align with tutorial.final.md. Optional.

shots.description: Shot-level storyboard decomposition: the renderable units within this section. REQUIRED-IN-SPIRIT whenever duration_hint_seconds > 15 (enforced by scripts/pipeline_lint.py as a hard error once the stage is approved/reviewed): cut the section into shots so the picture changes every <=~12s instead of one static frame. Each shot binds ONE component + props + the voice slice it covers + its own duration. Stage 07 maps each shot one-to-one to a data.ts scene; when shots is absent it falls back to the section-level scene_template/props. This supersedes visual_beats for cross-shot cutting (visual_beats stays for intra-shot micro-animation). See shared/docs/remotion-spec.md §1.5.

id.description: Shot id, unique within the section, e.g. '4.1'.

scene_template.description: The single Remotion component/template this shot renders, e.g. @TerminalScene.

props.description: Static props for this shot's component. Data must align with tutorial.final.md.

voice_slice.description: The exact substring of this section's voice that this shot covers (for voice<->picture alignment). In order, the slices should reconstruct the section voice. Optional but recommended.

duration_seconds.description: This shot's on-screen duration. The shot durations should sum to roughly the section duration_hint_seconds.

track.description: Per-shot track override (defaults to the section track).

visual_beats.description: Intra-shot micro-animation cues for THIS shot's single component (stagger / highlight / zoom). Not a substitute for cutting to a new shot.

animation_cues.description: Frame-based animation cues (alternative/legacy to visual_beats). Optional.

sub_shots.description: Anti-deadtime decomposition into <=15s sub-shots (alternative to visual_beats). See shared/docs/remotion-spec.md §1.5. Optional.

track.description: A-rail (Concept animation), B-rail (OS screen recording), or A/B (split) / A+B (composited) mix

visual_instructions.description: Visual scene prompt or Remotion code instructions

visual_beats.description: LEGACY anti-deadtime annotation timeline for the whole section. These are descriptive {at_seconds, action} strings, NOT renderable units (no component/props/voice slice), so they cannot drive real cross-shot cutting. Prefer shots[] for sections > 15s; keep visual_beats only as intra-shot micro-animation cues for a single component instance. The linter treats a >15s section that has only visual_beats (no shots) as a soft/legacy decomposition and warns to migrate. See shared/docs/remotion-spec.md §1.5.

at_seconds.description: Seconds from this section's start when the visual change happens

action.description: The visible change, e.g. 'stagger row in', 'zoom focus', 'highlight_cell', 'switch component', 'B-roll cut-in'

zoom_crop_directives.description: B-track screen-recording zoom/crop directives consumed by stage 05 (b-roll) and 07 (assembly). Merged in from the retired 03 stage; optional.

template_tickets.description: Remotion component extension tickets if new templates are needed. Merged in from the retired 03 stage; optional.

highlights_pitfall.description: Does the script explicitly show a failure moment/pitfall?

explains_boundary.description: Does the script define the boundary of what AI can/cannot do?

acceptance_standard.description: Does the script explain how the human verifies/accepts the code?
